source: graded from our own game-by-game scorecard

headline.line: Across 163 graded games this season, our predicted winning margin misses the final result by about 10.9 points on average, and we call the right winner 64.4% of the time.
headline.bias_line: No systematic lean: across the season our calls sit within a point of dead-center — we don't quietly favor home teams or road teams.
headline.caveat: The misses that hurt are blowouts — games that turn into runaways no one saw coming. About eleven points is close to the practical floor for a single WNBA game: past that, the result is mostly shooting luck that no model — or betting market — can predict. We publish the miss instead of hiding it.

backtest.heading: Is this number stale? We re-check it against every past game

metrics.label: Average margin miss, as we published it
Same games, re-scored with today's framework
metrics.note: Essentially identical — no hidden drift between what we shipped and what we'd ship now.

metrics.label: How often we call the right winner
metrics.note: Share of games where our favored side actually won.

calibration.label: Our 80% margin range contained the result
Our 90% margin range contained the result

backtest.takeaway: Re-running our current method on every past game reproduces almost exactly the accuracy we already published — so the headline above is what we actually shipped, not a number flattered after the fact. The framework is stable, not stale.
